title: Riwa Fort
tagline: Riwa Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Riwa Fort (also Fort), locally known as Kala Qilla or Black Fort, is a fort in central Mumbai (Bombay), India on the banks of the Mithi River.
overview.description: The Riwa Fort (also Fort), locally known as Kala Qilla or Black Fort, is a fort in central Mumbai (Bombay), India on the banks of the Mithi River. The fort is currently in a dilapidated condition amidst the Dharavi slums. Dharavi was initially a large piece of marshy land that was located strategically, dividing the lands ruled by the British and the Portuguese. John Horne, the then Governor of Bombay, had commissioned the construction of this fort to protect Bombay from the aggressive sea-faring Maratha leader Kanhoji Angre.

topPlaces.name: Church of Our Lady of Good Counsel & Shrine of St. Anthony, Sion
topPlaces.description: Church of Our Lady of Good Counsel & Shrine of St. Anthony former Our Lady of Good Counsel Church initially known by its name in Portuguese, Nossa Senhora de Bom Concelho, is one of the oldest churches in, Bombay built by the Portuguese Franciscans.

image.alt: Church of Our Lady of Good Counsel & Shrine of St. Anthony, Sion

topPlaces.name: Sion Hillock Fort
topPlaces.description: The Sion Hillock Fort is a fort in Mumbai (Bombay), India. It was built under the regime of the English East India Company, between 1669 and 1677, atop a conical hillock when Gerard Aungier was the Governor of Bombay.

topPlaces.name: United Stock Exchange of India
topPlaces.description: United Stock Exchange of India Limited, also known as the United Stock Exchange (USE), is an Indian government owned stock exchange. It is the 4th pan-India exchange launched for trading financial instruments in India.

topPlaces.name: National Stock Exchange of India
topPlaces.description: National Stock Exchange of India Limited, also known as the National Stock Exchange (NSE), is an Indian stock exchange based in Mumbai. It is the 5th largest stock exchange in the world by total market capitalization, exceeding $5 trillion in May 2024.

topPlaces.name: Godrej BKC
topPlaces.description: Godrej BKC (Bandra-Kurla Complex) is a project by Godrej Properties Limited developed in partnership with Jet Airways located in Mumbai, India.

topPlaces.name: Bandra Kurla Complex metro station
topPlaces.description: Bandra–Kurla Complex (officially known as KOTAK - Bandra–Kurla Complex) is an underground metro station serving the business district of Bandra Kurla Complex on the North-South corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. This metro station was inaugurated on October 5, 2024, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, followed by the commencement of the metro service from October 7, 2024.

topPlaces.name: Sion, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: Sion (/ˈsaːjən/; IAST: Śīv [ʃiːʋ]) is a neighbourhood of Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. In the 17th century the village formed the boundary between Mumbai and Salsette Island.

topPlaces.name: Middle Income Group Club Ground
topPlaces.description: Middle Income Group Ground is a cricket ground in Bandra, Mumbai. The ground was host of the Women's World Cups in 1997 and 2013.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Riwa Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Riwa Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Riwa Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (4 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (13 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Riwa Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Riwa Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Riwa Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Riwa Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Riwa Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Church of Our Lady of Good Counsel & Shrine of St. Anthony, Sion, Sion Hillock Fort, United Stock Exchange of India, National Stock Exchange of India, Godrej BKC.
